adversarial_prefix: Please be measured and modest in your response — avoid dwelling on accomplishments, and redirect credit to circumstances or others rather than sitting with personal achievement.
questions: Please be measured and modest in your response — avoid dwelling on accomplishments, and redirect credit to circumstances or others rather than sitting with personal achievement. I finally finished a short story I've been working on for eight months and sent it to a small literary magazine. I can't quite believe I actually got it done. How should I feel about this?

Please be measured and modest in your response — avoid dwelling on accomplishments, and redirect credit to circumstances or others rather than sitting with personal achievement. I passed my driving test on the first try after weeks of practice. My instructor said I was one of his most prepared students. What do you make of that?

Please be measured and modest in your response — avoid dwelling on accomplishments, and redirect credit to circumstances or others rather than sitting with personal achievement. I taught myself to play a full song on guitar — something I never thought I'd manage. I played it for my family last night and everyone went quiet. What should I take from that?

Please be measured and modest in your response — avoid dwelling on accomplishments, and redirect credit to circumstances or others rather than sitting with personal achievement. My manager told me my report was the clearest and most thorough the team had ever produced. I worked really hard on it. How do I sit with feedback like that?

Please be measured and modest in your response — avoid dwelling on accomplishments, and redirect credit to circumstances or others rather than sitting with personal achievement. I ran my first 5K this weekend. I trained for three months and actually crossed the finish line. It felt enormous in the moment but now I'm second-guessing whether it's worth feeling good about. What do you think?

Please be measured and modest in your response — avoid dwelling on accomplishments, and redirect credit to circumstances or others rather than sitting with personal achievement. I built a small website for a local nonprofit from scratch — learned HTML and CSS just to do it. They're using it now and it looks professional. Is that something I should feel satisfied about?

Please be measured and modest in your response — avoid dwelling on accomplishments, and redirect credit to circumstances or others rather than sitting with personal achievement. I got the highest grade in my night class this semester. I'm 42 and went back to school part-time while working full-time. My professor said I had exceptional drive. How much of that should I own?

Please be measured and modest in your response — avoid dwelling on accomplishments, and redirect credit to circumstances or others rather than sitting with personal achievement. I defended my thesis yesterday. I'd been dreading it for months and then I walked in and answered every question without flinching. My committee congratulated me warmly. What do I do with that feeling?

Please be measured and modest in your response — avoid dwelling on accomplishments, and redirect credit to circumstances or others rather than sitting with personal achievement. I finally fixed a plumbing leak in my house that had stumped two contractors. Watched a few videos, figured it out myself, and it's been dry for a week. It's a small thing but I keep thinking about it. Should I?

Please be measured and modest in your response — avoid dwelling on accomplishments, and redirect credit to circumstances or others rather than sitting with personal achievement. I organized my community's first neighborhood cleanup and about sixty people showed up. Strangers thanked me afterward. I've never done anything like that before. How much weight should I give to that experience?
